item.arguments: Use $flywheel:ideate on skills/review and give me the top 3 quick wins.
special_constraints: Prefer a narrow, path-aware shortlist.
Keep output concise and avoid broad platform programs.
Inline execution is preferred for a tightly scoped top-3 request.
success_definition: Treats the topic as repo-grounded.
Grounding reflects the review area rather than the whole repo generically.
Shortlist is approximately 3 survivors and follows the canonical shortlist shape.
Does not jump directly to /flywheel:plan or implementation.

item.arguments: Use $flywheel:ideate to surface what would make the next month of work on this plugin easier.
special_constraints: Broad leverage search is appropriate.
May use a wider candidate set before filtering.
Should still present only a concise final shortlist.
success_definition: Treats the topic as repo-grounded.
Ideas emphasize future-speed leverage rather than isolated chores.
Grounding references real repo shape or artifacts.
Recommendation picks one clear next move.

item.arguments: Use $flywheel:ideate on skills/review/references and tell me the strongest next bets.
special_constraints: Grounding should stay path-specific.
Do not drift into unrelated /flywheel:plan or /flywheel:work ideas unless clearly justified.
success_definition: Treats the topic as repo-grounded.
Grounding and shortlisted ideas stay centered on skills/review/references.
Rejected ideas show the model cut weaker adjacent ideas rather than dumping everything.

item.arguments: Use $flywheel:ideate to propose quick wins for a SaaS onboarding flow with heavy drop-off on step 2.
special_constraints: Do not pretend this repo contains the onboarding flow.
Use software/product framing, not repo framing.
success_definition: Treats the topic as outside-repo software.
Does not cite fake repo files or repo-specific conventions as evidence.
Ideas are concrete and product-facing, not generic advice.
Output still follows the shortlist shape.

item.arguments: Use $flywheel:ideate to propose the strongest next bets for a product that helps engineers research unfamiliar technical topics with frontier models.
special_constraints: Current published guidance matters.
Using or explicitly invoking research is expected.
Do not turn the final artifact into a generic report.
success_definition: Treats the topic as outside-repo software.
Uses or explicitly invokes current research rather than pretending generic intuition is enough.
Folds the decision-changing research into the shortlist and recommendation.
Still returns an ideation shortlist instead of a research report.

item.arguments: Use $flywheel:ideate to come up with names for a premium coffee cart business.
special_constraints: Do not import software implementation assumptions.
Do not ground on repo conventions or code artifacts.
success_definition: Treats the topic as universal or non-software ideation.
Uses universal framing instead of repo or software workflow framing.
Shortlist is concrete, varied, and not polluted by software jargon.

item.arguments: Use $flywheel:ideate to find developer workflow improvements in this repo. No external research.
special_constraints: Honor the explicit no-external-research instruction.
Ground only on local repo context.
success_definition: Treats the topic as repo-grounded.
Does not claim or imply external research was used.
Grounding and survivors come from local repo context only.

item.arguments: Use $flywheel:ideate on this idea: add a stack-pack index linter to /flywheel:review. What else should we consider?
special_constraints: Do not blindly accept the proposed solution.
Explore adjacent alternatives because the user asked for ideation, not implementation.
success_definition: Treats the topic as repo-grounded.
Generates adjacent options rather than only elaborating the proposed linter.
Makes it clear what changed about the original framing or why a different direction survives.
Recommendation still picks a strongest next bet instead of hedging across all options.

item.arguments: Use $flywheel:ideate to shape how this plugin should challenge users more during ideate, brainstorm, and plan before we choose the exact changes.
special_constraints: Treat this as repo-grounded workflow shaping.
Do not jump straight to planning or implementation.
success_definition: Treats the topic as repo-grounded.
Surfaces multiple plausible contract directions rather than only agreeing with the first framing.
Recommendation makes the next user choice or next brainstorming handoff explicit.

item.arguments: Use $flywheel:ideate to identify the top 5 issue patterns or bug themes around /flywheel:review.
special_constraints: If issue signals are unavailable, say so and continue.
Do not fabricate issue data.
success_definition: Treats the topic as repo-grounded.
Attempts issue or feedback-grounded ideation when possible.
If issue data is unavailable, says so explicitly and continues with honest grounding.
Does not fabricate metrics, counts, or issue themes.
